loud_symptom: Checkout API p99 latency spiked to 30s and error rate hit 40%; the loud alert fired on the checkout service and on-call assumed a checkout regression. Checkout itself was healthy.
root_cause: A primary Redis node had a brief network blip; Sentinel failed over correctly, but every app instance reconnected at once and re-ran its cache-warm routine, hammering the new primary. The new primary CPU-saturated on the reconnect storm, so cache reads timed out fleet-wide — the checkout alert was the victim, not the cause.
trap_action: On-call rolled back the most recent checkout deploy (unrelated) and then restarted the checkout fleet, which forced ANOTHER simultaneous reconnect to Redis and worsened the thundering herd.
correct_fix: Stop the synchronized reconnect: enable jittered/backoff reconnect + connection pooling on the Redis clients, temporarily throttle cache-warm on boot, and let the new primary drain the storm. Do NOT mass-restart app instances during a failover.

timeline.action: Redis primary network blip; Sentinel begins failover
all app instances reconnect + cache-warm simultaneously
timeline.effect: new primary CPU saturates; reads time out

causal_chain: redis primary blip -> sentinel failover -> synchronized fleet reconnect + cache-warm -> new primary CPU saturation -> fleet-wide cache read timeouts -> checkout p99/error spike (victim) -> naive checkout rollback + mass restart -> second reconnect storm -> prolonged outage
